title: Strings 1
description: Rust has two main string types: `String` (owned, heap-allocated, growable) and `&str` (a string slice, a reference to string data).
instructions: Fix the function to return a String instead of a string slice.
hints: String literals like "blue" are of type &str, not String.
You need to convert the &str to a String.
explanation: In Rust, `String` is an owned, heap-allocated string type, while `&str` is a borrowed string slice. String literals like `"hello"` are `&str` by default. To create a `String` from a `&str`, you can use `.to_string()`, `String::from("...")`, or the `.into()` method.
